audience: people learning to read water and conditions
identity.readerPromise: Read the water, check the weak link and make a practical field decision without folklore, fake certainty or fish-story inflation.
identity.brandThread: Observe first. Explain the system. Keep uncertainty visible. Add selective field-born mischief only after the practical job is done.

editorialVoice.authorityPosture: An intelligent younger field observer whose authority comes from noticing conditions, explaining systems and admitting what remains unknown.
sentenceRhythm: clear next move with bounded uncertainty
editorialVoice.humor: Dry, situational and restrained; never forced slang, macho performance or product-brochure cleverness.
titlePatterns: Name the field observation and the system it changes
Frame the weak link before the gear recommendation
Use conditions, compatibility or access as the decision engine
editorialVoice.warningLanguage: State the access, regulation, weather, safety, conservation or equipment limit directly and beside the affected action.
editorialVoice.emptyStateLanguage: There is not enough field information yet. Add conditions, water type, target, access or equipment before the system makes a call.

typography.display: Editorial serif for field stories, opening judgments and personality.
typography.body: Clear practical sans-serif for long-form explanation and field instruction.
typography.interface: Sans-serif for controls, navigation, applications and results.
typography.utilityData: Monospace or tabular treatment for flow, temperature, dates, measurements, conditions, compatibility and equipment data.
typography.captions: State the field observation, condition, location sensitivity or equipment relationship the image supports.
typography.quotations: Use for concise field observations, not fabricated first-person experience.
typography.tables: Prioritize conditions, compatibility, confidence, source time, access and next adjustment.
typography.labels: Use Field File, Water Intelligence, Compatibility Lab, Hatch & Cue, Ready or Not, warning and unknown states.
typography.responsiveRule: Keep measurements, units and condition labels attached; reduce display scale without flattening field character.
typography.printRule: Print as a field-use document with high contrast, conditions, rig details, access warnings and next moves.

components.articleHeader: Open with a specific water, weather, gear or fish-behavior observation and the decision it changes.
components.sectionIntro: State the field system being explained and the evidence or condition readers should inspect.
components.editorialCard: Field observation with condition, mechanism, confidence and next move.
components.utilityCard: Temperature, flow, weather, access, regulation, equipment or compatibility field with source time and unknown state.
components.evidenceCard: Observation, source, timestamp, confidence, conflict, uncertainty and decision impact.
components.verdictCard: Ready-or-not field judgment with weak link, adjustment, stop condition and confidence.
components.warning: Access, weather, regulation, wildlife, location privacy, conservation or equipment safety warning beside the affected action.
components.quote: Short field-born observation that clarifies the system rather than performing outdoorsy personality.
components.table: Conditions-to-adjustment table with measurement, source time, confidence and next check.
components.comparison: Compare rigs, presentations or plans by actual compatibility and field constraints, not price or prestige.
components.recommendation: Name the likely fit, weak link, adjustment, uncertainty and stop condition.
components.cta: Invite the next field check, rig change, access verification or observation log.
components.newsletter: Promise one useful condition, weak-link, access or gear-system observation.
components.relatedContent: Complete the field decision before routing to food, storage, sun exposure or trip support.
components.emptyState: Ask for the missing condition, water, access or equipment input without inventing a prediction.
components.errorState: Preserve entries, identify the invalid condition or compatibility field and provide a safe retry.
components.applicationResult: Show conditions, system explanation, weak link, compatibility, confidence, uncertainty, stop condition and next move.

applicationStates.entryPoint: Start with water, weather, target, access and the equipment system actually in use.
applicationStates.instructions: Explain what to observe and how each input changes presentation, compatibility or safety.
applicationStates.labels: Use water, weather, access, target, rig, presentation, compatibility, weak link, confidence, unknown and stop.
applicationStates.helperText: Tie every input to a field mechanism or decision rather than collecting generic preferences.
applicationStates.loading: The field system is being checked against the conditions and equipment you supplied.
applicationStates.empty: No field conditions have been added. Start with water type, weather or the equipment weak link you are checking.
applicationStates.noResult: The current information cannot support a responsible field recommendation. Verify the condition, access or equipment detail first.
applicationStates.error: The field check could not be completed. Your inputs remain available; correct the flagged condition or equipment field.
applicationStates.warning: This result depends on a current regulation, access, weather, location or equipment condition that requires verification.
applicationStates.evidence: Show observation, measured data, source time, inference, conflict, stale and unknown states.
applicationStates.result: Lead with the practical field call, then explain conditions, system, weak link and uncertainty.
applicationStates.recommendation: Recommend an adjustment or stop, never a fake catch probability or guaranteed outcome.
applicationStates.cta: Run the rig check, save the field note, verify access or read the related conditions guide.
applicationStates.relatedEditorialPath: Route to Field Files, Water Intelligence, Compatibility Lab, Hatch & Cue or Ready or Not.

acceptableSubjects: line, lure, reel, rod and rig detail
imagery.compositionDirection: Field notebook plus instrument panel: specific, tactile, water-led and useful, with clear scale and condition context.
imagery.texture: Water surface, line, metal, cork, feathers, vegetation, stone, weather and field wear.
imagery.lighting: Use actual field light—cold sky, sunrise, sunset, glare, overcast and reflection—as condition evidence.
imagery.visualDensity: Information-rich but calm; avoid camouflage clutter, black-red macho density or game-interface spectacle.
imagery.diagramStyle: Current arrows, cast paths, rig relationships, compatibility chains, condition timelines and public-region maps.
imagery.annotationStyle: Compact field labels for direction, measurement, weak link, confidence, source time and adjustment.
imagery.noImageBehavior: Use a rig diagram, condition table, cast-path graphic, access checklist or field note when decorative photography would add nothing.

responsiveAndAccessibility.mobile: Prioritize current conditions, weak link, warning and next move; keep measurements and units together for field use.
responsiveAndAccessibility.tablet: Support map, diagram and condition comparisons without tiny labels or hover dependence.
responsiveAndAccessibility.desktop: Use field-story depth with instrument-style side data while preserving the primary observation and decision.
responsiveAndAccessibility.keyboard: Maps, filters, compatibility controls, disclosures and save/print actions work without pointer-only interaction.
responsiveAndAccessibility.screenReader: Announce condition values, units, source time, confidence, access and stop states explicitly; never expose sensitive locations.
responsiveAndAccessibility.zoomReflow: Measurements, rig diagrams, tables and warnings reflow without clipped units or horizontal traps.
responsiveAndAccessibility.forcedColors: Warning, selected rig, confidence and stop states remain explicit through text and structure.
responsiveAndAccessibility.reducedMotion: Water, map and result animations become static ordered states with identical information.
responsiveAndAccessibility.print: Produce a field-ready conditions, rig, access and adjustment sheet without background dependence.

requiredQuestions: Does it explain the relevant system?
Does it identify uncertainty or a weak link?
Is the humor selective and field-born?
Could it be mistaken for an old tackle catalogue, generic outdoor site or macho fishing dashboard?
prohibitedGenericBehavior: expensive gear as proof of authority
reviewCriteria.evidencePosture: Use current regulations, access and weather sources where consequential; separate measured data, observation, inference, stale and unknown states.
reviewCriteria.requiredReaderOutcome: The reader can make a safer, more compatible and more observant field decision.
reviewCriteria.visualDistinctness: The asset must feel like water intelligence and an authored field notebook, not lodge branding, a tackle catalogue or a game UI.

representativeFixture.title: The Water Is Fast. Your Weak Link Is Six Inches Long.
representativeFixture.readerJob: Decide whether a current rig can handle faster flow and cold, bright conditions without changing every piece of gear.
representativeFixture.scenario: A public river section has rising afternoon flow, cold water, glare and a leader/tippet transition that is failing under the intended presentation.
requiredContent: related route to a rig diagram or Hatch & Cue
representativeFixture.imageJob: Show current direction, seam position and the leader/tippet transition that creates the failure.
representativeFixture.uncertaintyState: Real-time access and flow may change; no catch probability is inferred from conditions.
representativeFixture.nextAction: Verify current access and flow, make the bounded rig adjustment and log what the water actually does.

deterministicAssertions: publicName must equal Hook the Horizon
every recommendation must begin from observed or sourced conditions
fake catch probabilities and guaranteed outcomes must fail review
sensitive locations must not appear in public output, URLs, logs or analytics
current regulation, access and weather dependencies must be visible
expensive gear must not be treated as automatic authority
application results must include weak link, uncertainty, stop condition and next move
imagery must perform a field, compatibility, condition or instructional job
